sowing.method: Planting of nursery-grown seedlings or cuttings in shaded pits

seed_rate.notes: Optimal plant population of 2500-3000 seedlings per hectare.

sowing.soil_conditions: Deep, well-drained, acidic, humus-rich soils with pH 5.0-6.0 under partial shade trees.

irrigation.notes: Water twice a week during dry periods to ensure sapling root setting.
Simulated blossom showers (sprinkler irrigation) trigger uniform flowering.
Essential to prevent flower drop and ensure initial berry development.

fertilizer.method: Ring placement around the drip line
fertilizer.notes: Incorporate under leaf mulch layers in the plant basin.

fertilizer.method: Soil surface application below shade canopy
fertilizer.notes: Promotes healthy canopy recovery after monsoon heavy rains.

maturity_indicators: Berries turn bright red or crimson (cherry stage)
Berries yield slightly when pressed between fingers
harvest.harvest_method: Selective hand picking of ripe cherries
harvest.notes: Process harvested cherries immediately (wet pulping or dry sun drying) to avoid fermentation.
